steps.text: Let \(n\) = number of products sold. At zero sales the price is \$90, and each additional unit sold drops the price by \$1.50, so \(\text{price} = 90 - 1.5n\).
Revenue = price \(\times\) quantity: \((90 - 1.5n)n = 1336.50\). Expanding: \(90n - 1.5n^2 = 1336.50\). Rearranging: \(1.5n^2 - 90n + 1336.50 = 0\). Dividing by 1.5: \(n^2 - 60n + 891 = 0\).
Factor or use the quadratic formula. Discriminant: \(60^2 - 4(891) = 3600 - 3564 = 36\). So \(n = \frac{60 \pm 6}{2}\), giving \(n = 33\) or \(n = 27\).

• A: 27 — one of the two valid solutions (\(n = 27\)). That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: 30 — check: \(30(90 - 45) = 30 \times 45 = 1350 \neq 1336.50\). Eliminated.
  • C: 831 — far exceeds the price-zero break-even at \(n = 60\); price would be negative. Eliminated.
  • D: 1,350 — similarly impossible; price becomes deeply negative. Eliminated.
Note: \(n = 33\) is also a valid mathematical root of the equation, but it does not appear among the answer choices, so \(n = 27\) (choice A) is the only answer available.
